metadata.title: Build a Brand Negative Space Mapper with Claude
metadata.note: cli-ideas Card 19 — Ch 6 (Brand Strategy and Positioning). DOUBLE-CHECK: Stripe declined enterprise sales processes for first several years; broad small-business market in early marketing; rapid product proliferation; competitor-bashing content; shipping features before documentation was done. Stripe Atlas launched 2016 (~6 years after Stripe), Stripe Issuing 2018, Stripe Climate 2020. Pattern of sustained refusals over 15 years is the brand. Tag each refusal [VERIFIED-PUBLIC] or [INFERRED-FROM-PATTERN]. Consistency check fires if refusals contradict each other.

beats.role_note: COLD OPEN LAW — Claude UI, ask shown answered; IN-FOR-BEAR
beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Build it with Claude, then take it apart.

props.command: claude "map this brand's negative space — what it has consistently declined to build"
output: Positioning sentence derived from refusal pattern

beats.role_note: stakes — BEFORE CLI loop; SHOW-DON'T-TELL: Stripe refusals building up over 15 years
beats.narration_text: Read Stripe's marketing site, their blog, Patrick Collison's public writing. Then ask: what is Stripe's brand? Most people answer by listing what Stripe does. That is the wrong answer, because it describes output. What makes a brand legible across fifteen years and a dozen product lines is not what a company consistently produces. It is what a company consistently declines to produce. Enterprise sales processes declined for the first several years. Competitor-bashing content never shipped. Stripe Atlas launched in 2016 — six years after Stripe launched. Features held until the documentation was done. Each refusal, individually, looks like a one-time choice. The pattern is the brand. This tool reads the pattern.
beats.visual_intent: Timeline: Stripe 2010 launch → 2011 declines enterprise sales → 2012 documentation-first rule → 2016 Stripe Atlas (6yr gap) → 2018 Issuing → 2020 Climate; each gap annotated as 'declined to rush'; right panel: refusal list building up as the brand identity crystallizes; no composer yet

beats.role_note: ClaudeComposerAsk — show and discuss the prompt
beats.narration_text: Into Claude. Step one: Tactical Negative Space — list specific features, categories, or segments this brand has demonstrably declined to build. Tag each as verified-public or inferred-from-pattern. Step two: Strategic Negative Space — who would this brand have to stop being to serve them? Step three: a two-sentence profile synthesizing what the refusals collectively signal. Step four: a draft positioning sentence derived from the refusal pattern, not from the brand's own marketing copy. Step five: a consistency check — do the refusals form a coherent identity, or do some contradict each other? Format contradictions as: refusal A implies X, refusal B implies not X.

props.command:
claude "map negative space for [BRAND NAME]:
  Step 1 — Tactical Negative Space:
    specific features/categories/segments declined
    tag: [VERIFIED-PUBLIC] or [INFERRED-FROM-PATTERN]
  Step 2 — Strategic Negative Space:
    who would this brand have to stop being to serve them?
  Step 3 — Profile: 2-sentence synthesis
  Step 4 — Positioning sentence from refusal pattern
    (NOT from brand's own marketing copy)
  Step 5 — Consistency check:
    coherent identity, or contradictions?
    format: REFUSAL A implies X, REFUSAL B implies not X"

beats.role_note: ClaudeCodeBeat — actual mapper script; trimmed to what teaches
beats.narration_text: Read it. The source tag logic is the discipline: the model can infer absences from public patterns, but inferred absences are not documented decisions. The VERIFIED-PUBLIC tag requires an actual public record. The INFERRED-FROM-PATTERN tag means the model noticed a sustained absence but cannot cite a source. The consistency check is the other critical output — a set of refusals that contradict each other means the brand is trying to be two things at once, which is the structural definition of incoherence.

props.title: negative_space.py
props.code:
# negative_space.py  —  Claude Code output
import anthropic, sys

SYSTEM = """You are a brand positioning analyst.
For [BRAND]:
1. Tactical Negative Space — declined features/categories:
   Tag: [VERIFIED-PUBLIC] or [INFERRED-FROM-PATTERN]
2. Strategic Negative Space:
   Who would this brand stop being to serve them?
3. Profile: 2-sentence synthesis of what refusals signal.
4. Positioning sentence — derived from refusal pattern.
   NOT from the brand's own marketing copy.
5. Consistency check:
   Do refusals form a coherent identity?
   Contradictions → format:
   'REFUSAL [A] implies [X],
    REFUSAL [B] implies [not X]'
Do NOT present inferred absences as documented decisions.
The positioning sentence is a draft; mark it DRAFT."""

client = anthropic.Anthropic()
result = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-opus-4-7", max_tokens=2048,
    system=SYSTEM,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": sys.stdin.read()}]
)
print(result.content[0].text)

beats.role_note: screen-rec or animated slot — Stripe negative space map printing
beats.narration_text: Run it on Stripe. Tactical negative space prints: enterprise sales processes for the first several years — verified-public; competitor-bashing content — verified-public; features shipped before documentation finished — inferred-from-pattern; rapid product proliferation — verified-public, Stripe Atlas six years in, Issuing eight years. Strategic negative space: small-business-first products, consumer billing. Profile: Stripe's refusals consistently exclude urgency, volume, and direct-to-consumer paths. Positioning sentence from the refusal pattern: infrastructure for technical builders who need reliability over accessibility. Consistency check: COHERENT.
beats.visual_intent: screen-rec mp4: tactical negative space table printing with [VERIFIED-PUBLIC] and [INFERRED-FROM-PATTERN] tags; strategic negative space as paragraph; profile in amber box; positioning sentence with DRAFT label; consistency: COHERENT in green.

beats.narration_text: Now apply the mapper to your own brand or project. The personal brand version of negative space is professional opportunities you have declined — a consulting role that would have put you in the wrong archetype, a client whose problem was outside your lane, a platform you left. Sustained refusals over time are a brand statement whether you wrote them down or not. The mapper makes them legible.

props.command:
claude "map negative space for my brand:
  [BRAND DESCRIPTION OR PERSONAL BRAND]
  What have I consistently declined:
    - features/products not built despite demand
    - audiences not served despite fit
    - opportunities declined for brand reasons
  Tag each [VERIFIED-PUBLIC] or [INFERRED-FROM-PATTERN]
  Derive positioning sentence from refusals.
  Run consistency check."

beats.role_note: screen-rec or animated slot — personal brand negative space with positioning sentence
beats.narration_text: The personal brand negative space prints a different kind of list: not product features declined, but roles, clients, and platforms. The positioning sentence derived from the refusal pattern often surprises — it reads as more specific and more distinctive than anything the person would have written about themselves from the positive side. That is the structural point: the refusals reveal the actual identity more clearly than the portfolio does.
beats.visual_intent: Two-panel: left — feature refusals (Stripe example); right — personal brand refusals (clients not taken, platforms left, roles declined); positioning sentences from each side-by-side; bottom: 'brand = sustained refusal pattern — more legible from the out-list than the in-list'

beats.narration_text: Brand strategy is the document that makes refusals explicit before they are forced. The Stripe inversion shows: each individual refusal could have been a one-time choice. The fifteen-year consistency is the brand. The negative space mapper reads that consistency and derives the positioning from it — which is a harder-to-copy identity signal than any feature list.
beats.visual_intent: Summary: Stripe refusal timeline compressed; bottom: 'a brand defined by what it declines is harder to copy than one defined by what it ships'

beats.role_note: HANDOFF LAW — prompt READ aloud AND discussed; greeting 'Your turn.'
beats.narration_text: Your turn. Think about what you have consistently declined — in your product, in your career, in your work. Paste a description of those refusals and run the mapper. Read the positioning sentence it derives from the pattern. If it surprises you, that is signal — the refusals reveal an identity that the portfolio may not be expressing clearly. The consistency check is equally important: if your refusals contradict each other, you are building two brands at once, and neither will cohere.

props.command:
claude "map negative space for [BRAND / PERSONAL BRAND]:
  Describe refusals and sustained absences:
    [WHAT YOU DECLINED]
  Derive tactical negative space + strategic negative space.
  Produce positioning sentence from pattern.
  Run consistency check — flag contradictions."

beats.narration_text: Build a Brand Negative Space Mapper with Claude. This is Liam, in for Bear — build it with Claude, then take it apart.
